title: CLIP Score as a Quality Signal
concept: CLIP can also evaluate a generated image's match to its prompt after generation, providing an automated quality metric called CLIP Score.

exercises.prompt: When you type a prompt for an AI image generator, how do you usually tell if the picture it made is actually good?

options.text: I just look at it and guess based on my own eyes
The AI already tells me a perfect score every time
There's no way to ever check it

exercises.explain: Right now humans usually judge by eye — but AI can actually measure this automatically using something called CLIP Score!

cards.title: Did the AI Actually Listen?
cards.body: You ask an AI to draw 'a red fox jumping over a fence at sunset.' It gives you a picture... but did it really follow your prompt, or did it just make a pretty image that ignores half your words? There's actually a number that can tell you!

cards.title: What is CLIP Score?
cards.body: CLIP Score is a number that measures how well a generated image matches its text prompt. CLIP looks at the image and the prompt together, sees how closely their meanings line up, and gives a higher score for a better match and a lower score for a poor match.

cards.title: Like a Grading Rubric
cards.body: Imagine a teacher grades how well your drawing matches an assignment description. If you were told to draw 'a blue house with a red roof' and you drew exactly that, you get a high grade. If you drew a green house instead, you get a lower grade. CLIP Score is like that teacher, but it's an AI grading images against prompts automatically.

cards.title: Comparing Two Images
cards.body: Prompt: 'a cat wearing a birthday hat.' Image A shows a cat wearing a colorful party hat — CLIP Score gives it a high score. Image B shows a cat with no hat at all — CLIP Score gives it a low score, because the image doesn't match the words in the prompt.

cards.title: Myth: A High CLIP Score Means a Perfect Image
cards.body: Not quite! CLIP Score only measures how well the image matches the prompt's meaning. An image could have a high CLIP Score but still look blurry, weird, or have extra fingers. CLIP Score checks 'matching,' not overall picture quality or realism.

cards.title: Two Jobs for CLIP
cards.body: CLIP isn't just for guiding image creation — it can also grade the finished result against the prompt.

left.text: CLIP helps guide the AI while it's creating the image, nudging it toward the prompt

right.text: CLIP Score checks the finished image and grades how well it matches the prompt

options.text: How many colors are in an image
How well a generated image matches its text prompt
How fast the AI generated the image
How large the image file is

exercises.explain: CLIP Score compares the meaning of the image and the prompt to see how well they match — that's its whole job!

exercises.prompt: A high CLIP Score guarantees the image is perfectly realistic with no visual mistakes.
exercises.explain: False! CLIP Score only measures prompt-matching, not whether the image looks realistic or has visual errors.

exercises.sentence: CLIP Score is a number that shows how well a generated image matches its text ___.
exercises.explain: CLIP Score measures how closely the image's content matches the meaning of the text prompt used to create it.

exercises.prompt: A prompt says 'a dog playing in the snow.' Which image would most likely get the HIGHEST CLIP Score?

options.text: An image of a dog jumping happily in a snowy field
An image of a cat sitting on a couch
An image of an empty desert with no animals

exercises.explain: The image that best matches the meaning of the words in the prompt — a dog playing in snow — gets the highest CLIP Score.

exercises.prompt: You generate two images from the prompt 'a robot riding a skateboard.' Image 1 clearly shows a robot on a skateboard. Image 2 shows a robot standing still with no skateboard. What would CLIP Score most likely say?

options.text: Both images get the exact same score since they both show a robot
Image 1 gets a higher score because it matches the full prompt better
Image 2 gets a higher score because standing robots are easier to draw
CLIP Score can't be used to compare two images

exercises.explain: CLIP Score compares each image's meaning to the prompt's meaning. Image 1 matches more of the prompt (robot AND skateboard AND riding), so it earns a higher CLIP Score than Image 2, which is missing the skateboard action.
